msgs.text:
I treated the prior turns as external proposals, not as proof. The problem format fixes the first two generators as the tame generators Пғ,П„, with the tame relation П„Пғ=П„2 implicit, and all later generators in the pro-2 core; the prior logвҖҷs only still-live candidate is the Turn 9вҖ“29 вҖңdynamic tame-inertiaвҖқ word.

The surviving candidate is still:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ПҖ=ПҖ2вҲҲZ and О·=2ПҖ on prime-to-2 tame inertia,r=xПғвҲ’ПҖxПғПҖxвҲ’1(xП„вҲ’О·)ПҖ ((yП„)ПҖ)ПғyвҲ’1,with constants ПҖвүЎ40491355905,вҲ’ПҖвүЎ45176306175,вҲ’О·=вҲ’2ПҖ вүЎ50932630783(mod85667662080).The literature boundary remains as in prior turns: LabuteвҖҷs DemuЕЎkin classification is the right invariant package for maximal pro-p Galois groups of local fields, while the known explicit local absolute Galois presentations and dyadic exceptions do not directly settle K=Q2.Regensburg Pub Server+3 Cambridge University Press & Assessment+3 Tel Aviv University Math Department+3 My main new contribution this turn is a correction to the Turn 28вҖ“29 sign-kernel algebra. The conclusion those turns wanted is salvageable, but the displayed geometric-series coefficient must be reversed.

Let Ve,f=kerОә be the sign double cover of the candidateвҖҷs pro-2 kernel over a finite tame quotientО”e,f=вҹЁПғЛү,П„ЛүвҲЈП„Лүe=1,ПғЛүf=1,ПғЛүП„ЛүПғЛүвҲ’1=П„Лү2вҹ©,eВ odd,В eвҲЈ2fвҲ’1.

On a nontrivial tame-inertia character block, let П„ act by ОұоҖ =1, and let t be the deck involution of the sign cover. For the norm wordNa (u)=(uП„a)ПҖ,the Fox coefficient is the completed geometric sumDa=j=0вҲ‘ПҖвҲ’1(tОұa)j=1вҲ’tОұa 1вҲ’(tОұa)ПҖ.

Since ПҖвүЎ1(mod2) and ПҖвүЎ0(mode),(tОұa)ПҖ=tПҖОұaПҖ=t.

Therefore the correct formula isDa=1вҲ’tОұa1вҲ’t.This is the opposite of the expression implicitly used in part of Turn 29. The correction matters: 1вҲ’t is in the radical of the 2-adic sign-cover group algebra, whereas 1вҲ’tОұa is a unit because ОұaоҖ =1. HenceDaвҲҲ(1вҲ’t),and the coefficient contributed byxвҲ’1(xП„вҲ’О·)ПҖisвҲ’1+DвҲ’О·,which is a unit. Thus every nontrivial odd tame-inertia character block still has unit Smith pivots. So the desired conclusion survives, but only with this corrected denominator/numerator order.There is a second bookkeeping point to fix for any final write-up. In the trivial tame-inertia block, withA=F2 [T]/(TfвҲ’1),sвүЎПҖ(modf),the linear relation is(1+TвҲ’s)X+(1+T)Y=0.

If f=2a m with m odd, then sвүЎ1(mod2a) and sвүЎ0(modm). When f is even, s is odd, so 1+TвҲЈ1+TвҲ’s.

The correct elimination is therefore

Y=Q(T)X+YЛү,Q(T)=1+T1+TвҲ’s.Using the reciprocal is not legitimate in A. With this corrected Q, the reduced X-block multiplier is h(T)=1+Q(T)+Q(TвҲ’1).

For a nontrivial f-th root О¶,(1+О¶)h(О¶)=О¶вҲ’s+О¶s+1.

Thus h(О¶)=0 would forceО¶2s+1=1.But 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modm), hence gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Therefore О¶=1, contradiction. Also h(1)=1. So the trivial block remains nonsingular.Putting these two corrected calculations together gives a cleaner version of the sign-kernel statement:(kerОә)abвү…Z22ef+1вҠ•C4 for every finite tame (e,f), conditional on the prior finite-tame DemuЕЎkin/cup-product computation. The nontrivial tame-character blocks contribute only unit pivots; the unique C4 comes from the П„-invariant unramified block.This strengthens the audit of the live candidate, but it does not close the final problem. The decisive missing point is still not another Smith-form computation. The prior low-depth deformation warnings are real: knowing the oriented DemuЕЎkin kernel, its sign class, and its cyclotomic branch behavior does not by itself determine the exact filtered (Пғ,П„)-action. A proof still needs a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem identifying the candidateвҖҷs exact actionвҖ”includingПғПҖ=ПғsS (1вҲ’s)/f in unramified quotients and the dynamic tame correction П„вҲ’2ПҖ вҖ”with the arithmetic Frobenius/tame-inertia action over every finite tame subextension.

ESTABLISHED: The Turn 9вҖ“29 candidate remains the only unrefuted presentation candidate. I found and repaired a real algebraic slip in the sign-kernel proof: the correct nontrivial tame-character Fox coefficient isDa=(1вҲ’t)/(1вҲ’tОұa),not its reciprocal. With this correction, nontrivial tame-inertia blocks still have unit Smith pivots, and the uniform sign-kernel conclusion (kerОә)abвү…Z22ef+1вҠ•C4 survives. I also fixed the trivial-block elimination to

Q(T)=(1+TвҲ’s)/(1+T).

GAP: The candidate is still not a proved presentation of GQ2. The remaining gap is the full dyadic formation/rigidity step: one must prove that the candidateвҖҷs exact filtered tame action is the arithmetic action, not merely that each finite tame kernel has the correct oriented DemuЕЎkin and cyclotomic invariants.

NEXT: Formulate the missing theorem as an exact marked-operator rigidity statement for (Ue,f,ПҮ,S,ПғПҖ,П„вҲ’2ПҖ), then prove or disprove it by comparing the full filtered action with explicit Kummer/Witt arithmetic over finite tame extensions.[BREAKTHROUGH]
